zotero.abstractNote: Probabilistic numerical methods for ordinary differential equations (ODEs) provide a principled way to quantify uncertainty arising from numerical discretization. When used for parameter estimation in ODE-based models, they allow for a joint treatment of measurement noise and discretization error. However, the resulting posterior distributions are often highly non-convex and multimodal, making standard inference techniques like Markov chain Monte Carlo (MCMC) prone to getting stuck in local optima. We propose diffusion tempering, a novel sampling strategy that combines tempering with a diffusion-based transition kernel to improve exploration of the posterior. We demonstrate that diffusion tempering significantly improves the performance of probabilistic integrators for parameter estimation on a range of challenging ODE models, including systems with chaotic dynamics and high-dimensional parameter spaces.

extra.Editors: Salakhutdinov, Ruslan and Kolter, Zico and Heller, Katherine and Weller, Adrian and Oliver, Nuria and Scarlett, Jonathan and Berkenkamp, Felix

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 41st International Conference on Machine Learning
